title: Making File Names Unique
description:
Given an array of strings names of size n. You will create n folders in your file system such that, at the ith minute, you will create a folder with the name names[i].

Since two files cannot have the same name, if you enter a folder name that was previously used, the system will have a suffix addition to its name in the form of (k), where, k is the smallest positive integer such that the obtained name remains unique.

Return _an array of strings of length_ n where ans[i] is the actual name the system will assign to the ith folder when you create it.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def maxNumberOfFamilies(n, reservedSeats):
    rows = {}
    for seat in reservedSeats:
        rows[seat[0]] = rows.get(seat[0], 0) | (1 << (seat[1] - 1))
    
    max_groups = (n - len(rows)) * 2
    for row_mask in rows.values():
        seats = ~(row_mask | (row_mask >> 1) | (row_mask >> 2) | (row_mask >> 3)) & 0x3FF
        max_groups += (seats & (seats >> 1) & (seats >> 2) & (seats >> 3)) != 0
        
    return max_groups
```
    
    The algorithm uses bitwise operations to keep track of reserved and unreserved seats. It maintains a map (or dictionary in Python) to store the row number as the key and a bitmask representing the reserved seats as the value. For each reserved seat, it calculates the corresponding bitmask and updates the row's bitmask using bitwise OR operation.

Next, it calculates the maximum number of 4-person groups that can be formed in completely unreserved rows. This is equal to twice the number of unreserved rows (since two 4-person groups can be formed in each row). It initializes max_groups with this value.

It then iterates through each reserved row and its bitmask. To find available spots for a group, it calculates a bitmask of unreserved groups of seats each 4 contiguous empty seats. It does this using bitwise AND and NOT operations. Finally, it checks if there are any such groups in the row and increments the max_groups if there are.

At the end of the algorithm, max_groups store the maximum number of 4-person groups that can be seated.
